rationale: The cited paper develops a two-dimensional satisfaction construct (level plus strength/uncertainty via JUMP), and the citing paper’s core contribution is likewise to decompose satisfaction into level and heterogeneity and test how heterogeneity moderates the payoff of satisfaction. This close construct and modeling overlap makes the citation plausibly foundational/method-relevant rather than incidental. With a confirmed DOI match (10.1509/jmkr.44.1.153), this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
